prompt: I have 50 labeled support tickets with priority levels (critical, high, normal, low). I want to extract the decision rules so I can document them for my team. What DSPy optimizer should I use?
expected_output: Uses dspy.InferRules to extract interpretable classification rules
assertions: Defines a metric function with the (example, prediction, trace=None) signature
Calls optimizer.compile(program, trainset=trainset) to run optimization
Shows how to inspect the discovered rules via predictor.signature.instructions
Mentions that rules can be read, edited, or removed before deploying

prompt: I need my AI content moderation system to be explainable for compliance. My manager wants to see the exact rules the system follows. I have labeled moderation examples.
expected_output: Uses InferRules for explainable moderation with auditable rules
assertions: Uses dspy.InferRules for interpretability and auditability
Explains that InferRules produces human-readable rules appended to instructions
Shows how to extract and display the discovered moderation policy after compilation
Recommends providing a separate valset for better evaluation control
Does not use BootstrapFewShot or MIPROv2 since interpretability is the requirement

prompt: I tried InferRules on my creative writing task and the output quality got worse. What went wrong and what should I use instead?
expected_output: Explains InferRules limitations and recommends alternatives
assertions: Explains that InferRules works best on tasks with consistent patterns (classification, routing, triage)
States that creative or open-ended tasks have no consistent rules to discover
Recommends BootstrapFewShot or MIPROv2 as alternatives
Suggests comparing InferRules accuracy against a baseline to verify it actually helps
